evals.prompt: Hey, something seems off with the agent-stream edge function — users are reporting that their sessions aren't completing properly. Can you check what's going on?
evals.expected_output: Claude runs the fetch-logs.js script for the agent-stream function, fetches both runtime and edge logs, and provides an analysis of what it found — including any errors, status codes, latency patterns, or suspicious log messages.

assertions.name: runs_fetch_logs_script
assertions.description: Claude invokes the fetch-logs.js script via bash

assertions.name: filters_by_function_name
assertions.description: Claude passes --function agent-stream to narrow results

assertions.name: provides_analysis
assertions.description: Claude explains what was found in the logs, not just dumps raw output
assertions.criteria: The response interprets the logs and explains patterns or issues, rather than just reproducing the raw output

evals.prompt: I'm getting a 500 error back from ai-chat-completion when I send certain messages. It's been happening for the last couple hours. What's the error?
evals.expected_output: Claude fetches logs for ai-chat-completion, looks at both runtime errors and HTTP 500 responses in the edge logs, and identifies the error message or stack trace causing the 500s.

assertions.name: uses_sufficient_time_window
assertions.description: Claude uses at least a 2h time window given the user said 'last couple hours'
assertions.criteria: The fetch-logs command uses --last 2h or larger (e.g. 6h, 12h) rather than the default 30m

assertions.name: reports_on_errors
assertions.description: Claude explicitly mentions what errors or HTTP status codes it found
assertions.criteria: The response mentions specific error messages, status codes, or confirms that no errors were found in the logs

evals.prompt: Can you pull the logs for all edge functions for the last hour and give me a summary of what's been happening?
evals.expected_output: Claude runs the script without --function filter, fetches all functions' logs, and produces a summary covering which functions were called, error rates, any notable runtime messages.

assertions.name: no_function_filter
assertions.description: Claude does NOT use --function to restrict to a single function
assertions.criteria: The fetch-logs command does not include a --function flag, since the user asked for all functions

assertions.name: produces_summary
assertions.description: Claude summarizes activity across multiple functions, not just one
assertions.criteria: The response provides a meaningful summary covering multiple edge functions or the overall traffic/error picture
